title: Add Node.js/npm install to Phase 0 workspace setup
description: 03-real-world-skills/02-working-with-apis.md:38 runs 'npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem' with zero prerequisite install. Phase 0 only covers Rust/cargo, not Node.js/npm. A novice reaching the APIs chapter will either not have npm at all or will not know what 'npm' or '-g' means. Fix: Either (a) add a Node.js section to 00-foundations/04-your-workspace.md alongside the Rust section, pointing at nodejs.org LTS installer, showing 'node --version' and 'npm --version' verification; or (b) add a just-in-time install block at the top of 03-real-world-skills/02-working-with-apis.md explaining how to install Node.js before any npm command is shown, and what -g means (global install vs local project install).
